reasoning: Elon Musk announced the formation of the "America Party" on July 5, 2025, via a tweet following a public falling out with Donald Trump over a tax and spending bill. Despite this initial announcement, no concrete steps required by the resolution criteria—such as launching an official website, creating an official dedicated social media account, or filing paperwork—have materialized. A prominent X account with 300,000 followers explicitly states it has "No official affiliation". Furthermore, an FEC filing in July was discovered to be fake, leading Musk to deny official registration, and it was revealed he did not secure essential domain names or handles. Since July, media coverage on the topic has completely dropped off, suggesting it was an impulsive statement rather than an actionable plan. With the Manifold market probability sitting at 0.05 in mid-October and no evidence of actual party infrastructure by late October, it is highly unlikely Musk will fulfill the definitive criteria for establishing a political party by the end of 2025. I am forecasting at 3%.
